entries.caption: Donut chart of the churn label. The hollow ring shows the proportional split; the centre displays the total count and overall churn rate. Green = retained, red = churned.

Pearson correlation matrix across numeric features. Red = negative correlation, blue = positive correlation, neutral background = near-zero. Values inside the cells are the correlation coefficients. Pairs with |r| ≥ 0.85 are flagged as redundant and are candidates for dropping.

Churn rate per bucket of tenure_months. Orange dashed = overall rate (16.8%). Red dotted = 2.0× hotspot threshold. Red bars have cleared the hotspot line — candidate segments for a binary flag in Stage 3.2.

Churn rate per bucket of city_tier. Orange dashed = overall rate (16.8%). Red dotted = 2.0× hotspot threshold. Red bars have cleared the hotspot line — candidate segments for a binary flag in Stage 3.2.

Churn rate per bucket of preferred_payment_mode. Orange dashed = overall rate (16.8%). Red dotted = 2.0× hotspot threshold. Red bars have cleared the hotspot line — candidate segments for a binary flag in Stage 3.2.
Distribution of 2 categories across 5,630 customers. Pie chart chosen for readability at this category count.

Each bar spans 100% of customers inside that category. Red = churn rate. Orange dashed line = overall retention baseline — any category whose red segment extends PAST this line churns more than average (a hotspot).
Distribution of 3 categories across 5,630 customers. Horizontal bar chart chosen for readability at this category count.

Each bar spans 100% of customers inside that category. Red = churn rate. Orange dashed line = overall retention baseline — any category whose red segment extends PAST this line churns more than average (a hotspot).

Histogram of tenure_months across 5,630 customers. Orange = mean, purple = median. The gap between them confirms skew direction: mean > median ⇒ right-skewed, mean < median ⇒ left-skewed.

Box plot reads as: box = 25th–75th percentile (the middle 50%), line inside box = median, whiskers extend to 1.5 × IQR, dots beyond = outliers. Large vertical separation between the two medians = strong churn predictor. Box overlap that is still high means the feature alone does NOT distinguish the two groups.
